product_overview: CloudVault is a lightweight cloud file management backend that proxies uploads/downloads through Telegram (TDLib) while exposing APIs for uploads (single & chunked), downloads with caching and repair, thumbnails, Telegram auth/session lifecycle, and admin diagnostics. It supports guest sessions, real-time sync, and admin tooling for storage and TDLib maintenance.
core_goals: Provide reliable file upload (single and chunked) and download APIs backed by Telegram/TDLib with server-side caching and repair flows.
Expose robust thumbnail, delete, and Telegram auth/session utilities to manage Telegram-backed storage lifecycle.
Offer operational endpoints for health, metrics, storage cleanup, and TDLib optimization for observability and maintenance.
Support guest mode and real-time sync primitives so clients can operate without full user auth and receive live updates.
Minimize data loss and provide clear error states for upload/download failures (including chunked session resilience).

features.name: Health and Service Status
features.description: Reports runtime service health, TDLib readiness, active session capacity, uptime and timestamp to support monitoring and sanity checks.
user_flows: GET /health -> Receive 200 with {status, tdlib_ready:true, active_sessions, max_sessions, uptime, timestamp} (happy path)
GET /health -> Receive 200 with {status:'degraded', tdlib_ready:false, active_sessions, max_sessions, uptime, timestamp} when TDLib is not ready (degraded state)
GET /health -> Receive 500 when core service boot failure prevents health evaluation (error path)

features.name: Single File Upload
features.description: Accepts small/medium files via multipart/form-data, streams to Telegram via TDLib, and returns stored metadata including a remote/telegram file identifier.
user_flows: POST /api/upload/ with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and multipart/form-data file -> Receive 200 with file metadata including remoteFileId / telegram_file_id (happy path)
POST /api/upload/ without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized (error - auth missing)
POST /api/upload/ with file exceeding size limits -> Receive 413 Payload Too Large (error - size limit)

features.name: Chunked (Large) Uploads
features.description: Provides chunked upload lifecycle APIs: initialize session, upload chunks, poll status, and trigger server-side merge/finalization (async or single-call complete).
user_flows: POST /api/chunked-upload/init with JSON {name, size, contentType} -> Receive 200 with {uploadId, chunkSize, expiresAt} (init happy path)

POST /api/chunked-upload/chunk with uploadId and chunk bytes -> Receive 200 acknowledging chunk saved -> GET /api/chunked-upload/status?uploadId=<id> -> Receive 200 with {uploadedChunks, totalChunks} -> POST /api/chunked-upload/complete-start with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and uploadId -> Receive 202 Accepted with jobId -> GET /api/chunked-upload/complete-status?jobId=<jobId> -> Receive 200 with {status:'completed'} -> GET /api/chunked-upload/complete-result?jobId=<jobId> -> Receive 200 with final file metadata including remoteFileId (full happy path)
POST /api/chunked-upload/chunk with invalid or expired uploadId -> Receive 404 Upload session not found (error path)
POST /api/chunked-upload/complete without Authorization or with insufficient permissions -> Receive 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden (error path)

POST /api/chunked-upload/complete with all chunks included (single-call complete) and Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 with final file metadata including remoteFileId (single-call completion happy path)

features.name: File Download and Repair
features.description: Streams files retrieved from TDLib with support for Range requests, signed-public URLs, local cache status, and a repair endpoint to refresh stale Telegram references.
user_flows: GET /api/download/:remoteFileId with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and optional Range header -> Receive 206 Partial Content (for range) or 200 and streamed bytes (for full download) (happy path streaming)
GET /api/dl/:remoteFileId (public signed URL) with valid HMAC signature in query -> Receive 200 and streamed bytes (public signed URL happy path)
GET /api/dl/:remoteFileId (public signed URL) with invalid or tampered HMAC -> Receive 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden (signed URL validation error)
GET /api/download/status/:remoteFileId with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 with cache state (e.g. {cached:true, localPath, lastUpdated}) (status happy path)

POST /api/download/repair with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body {remoteFileId} -> Receive 202 Accepted with repairJobId -> GET /api/download/status/:remoteFileId -> Receive 200 showing refreshed cache or updated telegram reference (repair happy path)
GET /api/download/:remoteFileId with Authorization when remoteFileId does not exist -> Receive 404 Not Found (error path)

features.name: Thumbnail, Delete, and Session Utilities
features.description: Provides thumbnail retrieval/creation, Telegram message deletion/cleanup, and Telegram auth lifecycle endpoints for sending/validating codes and disconnecting sessions.
user_flows: GET /api/thumbnail/:remoteFileId with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 with thumbnail image bytes (happy path)
POST /api/thumbnail/from-message with Authorization and JSON {chatId, messageId} -> Receive 200 with generated thumbnail metadata (build-from-message happy path)
GET /api/thumbnail/:remoteFileId with Authorization for non-thumbnailable file -> Receive 415 Unsupported Media Type (error path)
DELETE /api/message/:chatId/:messageId with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 confirming deletion (message delete happy path)
POST /api/message/cleanup with Authorization and JSON {since, target} -> Receive 200 with cleanup summary (cleanup happy path)
POST /api/telegram/send-code with Authorization and JSON {phoneNumber} -> Receive 200 with {sent:true, phoneNumber} (send-code happy path)
POST /api/telegram/verify-code with Authorization and JSON {phoneNumber, code} -> Receive 200 with {sessionId, userId} when code valid (verify-code happy path)
POST /api/telegram/verify-code with bad code -> Receive 401 Unauthorized (verify-code error path)
POST /api/telegram/verify-password with Authorization and JSON {userId, password} -> Receive 200 with session when 2FA required and password valid (2FA happy path)
GET /api/telegram/status/:userId with Authorization -> Receive 200 with session/state details (status happy path)
POST /api/telegram/disconnect with Authorization and JSON {userId} -> Receive 200 confirming session removed (disconnect happy path)

features.name: Admin and Diagnostics
features.description: Admin endpoints for storage stats, targeted and full cleanup of artifacts, runtime metrics, cache clearing, and TDLib optimizeStorage operations for operational maintenance.
user_flows: GET /api/admin/storage/stats with Authorization: Bearer <AdminJWT> -> Receive 200 with storage usage and cache details (stats happy path)
POST /api/admin/storage/cleanup with Authorization: Bearer <AdminJWT> and JSON {target:'uploads', olderThan:'2026-01-01'} -> Receive 200 starting cleanup and a jobId (targeted cleanup happy path)
POST /api/admin/storage/uploads/cleanup with Authorization: Bearer <AdminJWT> -> Receive 200 with cleanup summary (uploads cleanup happy path)
POST /api/admin/storage/cleanup/all with Authorization: Bearer <AdminJWT> -> Receive 200 or 202 with jobId (full cleanup happy path)
POST /api/admin/cache/clear with Authorization: Bearer <AdminJWT> -> Receive 200 confirming cache cleared (cache clear happy path)
POST /api/admin/tdlib/optimize with Authorization: Bearer <AdminJWT> -> Receive 200 confirming TDLib optimizeStorage scheduled (optimize happy path)
GET /api/admin/metrics with Authorization: Bearer <AdminJWT> -> Receive 200 with runtime metrics (metrics happy path)
GET /api/admin/logs with Authorization: Bearer <AdminJWT> -> Receive 200 with recent logs (logs happy path)
Any admin endpoint with non-admin credentials -> Receive 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden (admin auth error path)
POST /api/admin/storage/cleanup/all -> Receive 500 Internal Server Error when cleanup job fails due to underlying IO errors (cleanup failure error path)

features.name: Guest Mode APIs
features.description: Support for unauthenticated guest users via a client-generated guest_session_id; guest-scoped file operations allow uploads and browsing tied to the session token but restrict cross-device sync and share-link generation.
user_flows: Client generates guest_session_id (client-side) -> POST /api/files with header X-Guest-Session: <guest_session_id> and multipart/form-data file -> Receive 200 with file metadata tagged with guest_session_id (guest upload happy path)
GET /api/files?guest_session_id=<guest_session_id> -> Receive 200 with list of files belonging to that guest_session_id (guest list happy path)
POST /api/share with Authorization: none and header X-Guest-Session: <guest_session_id> -> Receive 403 Forbidden (guests cannot create public share links) (guest-share error path)
POST /api/files without X-Guest-Session or Authorization -> Receive 401 Unauthorized (missing guest/session token error path)

features.name: Real-time Sync (Realtime Notifications)
features.description: Realtime notifications (e.g., Supabase Realtime) inform connected clients of file/folder changes so UIs can update immediately without polling.
user_flows: Subscribe to Supabase Realtime channel user:[user_id] (WebSocket) -> Receive realtime INSERT event for new file -> Client issues GET /api/files?userId=<user_id> to reconcile state -> Receive 200 with updated file list (happy path)

Subscribe to Supabase Realtime channel user:[user_id] -> Connection drops due to network -> Client attempts reconnect to Supabase Realtime channel -> If reconnect fails, client performs GET /api/files?userId=<user_id> as fallback -> Receive 200 with current state (reconnect fallback path)

features.name: Folder Management & File Preview
features.description: APIs for folder CRUD and moving files between folders; preview endpoints use thumbnail and partial-download endpoints to render previews in the client.
user_flows: POST /api/folders with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and JSON {name, parent_id} -> Receive 201 with folderId (create folder happy path)
PATCH /api/files/:fileId with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and JSON {folder_id:<folderId>} -> Receive 200 with updated file metadata (move file happy path)
PATCH /api/files/:fileId with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and JSON {folder_id:nonexistent} -> Receive 404 Not Found (move to non-existent folder error path)
GET /api/thumbnail/:remoteFileId with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 with thumbnail bytes for preview (image preview happy path)
GET /api/download/:remoteFileId?preview=true with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 with streamed preview bytes (PDF/video preview happy path)
GET /api/download/:remoteFileId?preview=true for unsupported type -> Receive 415 Unsupported Media Type (preview unsupported error path)

features.name: Health and Service Status
features.description: Reports service health, TDLib readiness, session capacity, and uptime.

endpoints.description: Returns service and TDLib health details.

features.name: File Upload API
features.description: Supports small direct uploads and large chunked uploads to Telegram-backed storage.

endpoints.description: Uploads a single file (multipart/form-data).
Initializes a chunked upload session.
Uploads one chunk to an active upload session.
Returns current chunked upload progress state.
Starts async chunk merge/finalization process.
Checks finalization job status.
Fetches finalization result and metadata.
Completes chunked upload in one request path.

features.name: File Download and Repair API
features.description: Downloads files via TDLib with cache, progressive/Range support, and fallback repair flows.

endpoints.description: Downloads or streams file bytes with optional Range support.
Public signed-URL download route using HMAC validation.
Returns TDLib local download/cache status for a file.
Repairs stale Telegram file references by forwarding messages.

features.name: Thumbnail, Delete, and Session Utilities
features.description: Handles preview thumbnails, Telegram message deletion cleanup, and Telegram auth lifecycle.

endpoints.description: Retrieves thumbnail bytes for a Telegram-backed file.
Builds a thumbnail from Telegram message content.
Deletes a Telegram message for content cleanup.
Performs cleanup operations for message/file artifacts.
Initiates Telegram login by sending verification code.
Verifies Telegram login code.
Verifies Telegram 2FA password when required.
Reports Telegram auth/session status for a user.
Disconnects and removes Telegram session state.

features.name: Admin and Diagnostics API
features.description: Exposes storage metrics and maintenance actions.

endpoints.description: Returns storage and cache usage stats.
Triggers targeted storage cleanup.
Cleans stale upload artifacts.
Runs full storage cleanup workflow.
Returns runtime metrics for diagnostics.
Clears in-process application cache.
Triggers TDLib optimizeStorage operation.
Retrieves recent logs for troubleshooting.

known_limitations.issue: Separate concurrency limiters in single upload and chunked upload paths are not unified.
known_limitations.impact: Throughput control may be inconsistent under mixed upload workloads.

known_limitations.issue: Upload deduplication is name-based and not content-hash enforced server-side.
known_limitations.impact: Duplicate content can be stored when filenames differ.

known_limitations.issue: Chunked upload session state is kept in memory only.
known_limitations.impact: In-progress uploads are lost on service restart.

known_limitations.issue: Thumbnail extraction can require full file download in certain flows.
known_limitations.impact: Increased latency and bandwidth usage for preview requests.
